question_text: Which of the following is an example of wasted energy in a traditional light bulb?

options.label: B
options.text: Heat energy released into the surroundings

correct_answer: Heat energy released into the surroundings
explanation: Traditional light bulbs convert electrical energy into both light and heat. While light is useful, the heat released into the surroundings is wasted energy because it doesn't help the bulb do its job of producing light. LED bulbs are more efficient because they produce more light and waste less energy as heat.
